title: fam-exp-poly / fam-horner-list — the POLYNOMIAL EVALUATION ENGINE on the native asm lane: a degree-(n-1) Horner over a self-contained f64 pool, coefficients as a Form LIST (DATA), emits four-way as Form->asm bytes AND runs bit-exact on the M4; the exp(r) core, the missing middle third of fam-exp
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: form-native-models.form 'GAPS — LLM INFERENCE': the transcendental ops as form-asm bytes. exp(x) = 2^k * exp(r). Every exp-lane PRIMITIVE was on bytes — k=round (form-asm-frintn 7), the range reduction to r (form-asm-exp-reduce 31), the raw 2^k scaling (form-asm-pow2 63) — but the exp(r) EVALUATOR itself was still only a degree-1 pooled fma (form-asm-exp-coef-pool / fam-pool-fma). A real exp(r) needs a degree-6 Horner, and there was no engine to emit one. fam-pow2's own receipt named this exact stone: 'a pooled-coefficient Horner exp(r)'. This brick supplies the general engine (any degree, coefficients as a list) and its exp instance — the middle third of a full native exp, between fam-exp-reduce (which yields r) and fam-pow2 (2^k).
core_lift: fam-horner-list is a CORE-LIFT, not a new special case. fam-pool-fma proved a SINGLE pooled fma (degree 1, a fixed 2-entry pool); fam-horner-list generalizes it to ANY degree in ONE engine — the coefficients are a Form list (DATA), so exp(r), sin, cos, gelu, silu are all instances of the same recipe over different pooled coefficient lists (no parallel per-transcendental code path). The 'generalize fam-pool-fma to a list' the exp-lane receipts kept naming.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk :: (defn fam-horner-list (coeffs) (append (fa-ldr-d-lit 1 (mul 2 (len coeffs))) (append (fhl-tail (tail coeffs) (add (mul 2 (len coeffs)) 1)) (fhl-pool coeffs)))) with helpers fhl-tail (emits the n-1 trailing (ldr d2 ; fmadd) pairs then ret; the FINAL fmadd targets d0, the rest d1) and fhl-pool (concatenates f64-bytes of every coefficient). ABI: d0 = x, returns d0 = p(x); the accumulator lives in d1 so x is preserved in d0 across every fma, and the final fmadd writing d0 removes the trailing fmov (making the code an even 2n words so the pool is 8-aligned). fam-exp-poly = (fam-horner-list (list 1/720 1/120 1/24 1/6 1/2 1.0 1.0)), the degree-6 Taylor evaluator of exp(r).
layout_math: For n coefficients the code is 2n words (56 bytes for n=7), the pool 8n bytes appended after ret. Each coefficient loads via a PC-relative literal (fa-ldr-d-lit) whose imm19 is the WORD distance to its pooled slot: 2n for the leading load and a CONSTANT 2n+1 for EVERY tail load — because each tail step advances 2 words in code (ldr + fmadd) AND 2 words in the pool (a .quad is 2 words), so the distance is invariant. Pinned against the M4 assembler oracle BEFORE authoring: the first otool read showed imm19 = [14,15,15,15,15,15,15] for n=7, correcting an initial (wrong) assumption of a 1-word pool stride. The pool bytes are computed by f64-bytes, so ANY value fills the pool — including 1/720 = 0x3f56c16c16c16c17, an irregular mantissa no repeating-nibble hand pattern writes.

proof_four_way.oracle: M4-assembled bytes (clang -c on a hand-written .s with the exact ldr-literal/fmadd sequence + a same-section 7-quad literal pool, __text parsed out of the Mach-O): 112 bytes = 56 code (ldr d1,+14 ; [ldr d2,+15 ; fmadd d1,d1,d0,d2] x5 ; ldr d2,+15 ; fmadd d0,d1,d0,d2 ; ret) + a 56-byte pool (.quad 1/720,1/120,1/24,1/6,1/2,1,1). A separate degree-2 (n=3) oracle (48 bytes, imm19 6/7/7) pins the generalization.
claims: 1 — the degree-6 exp program is 112 bytes = 56 code + 56 pool (len == 112)
2 — the FULL 112-byte program byte-equals the M4 degree-6 assembler oracle (fa-conviction == 1)
4 — the layout math GENERALIZES: a degree-2 (n=3) Horner byte-equals its own M4 oracle, imm19 2n / 2n+1 (fa-conviction == 1)
8 — coefficients are DATA: f64-bytes fills the pool with 1/720's irregular mantissa (23 108 193 22 108 193 86 63) exactly (fa-conviction == 1)
proof_four_way.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/format-arith.fk form-stdlib/f64-bytes.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-asm-exp-poly-band.fk
proof_four_way.result: -> 15 ; fourth arm four-way (fkwu, bootstrap binary darwin-arm64, no clang) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu agree on the 112-byte degree-6 image, on the degree-2 generalization, and on the f64-bytes pool. Crossed FIRST try on all four kernels (the recursion is a positional flatten the kernels compute identically; the offset arithmetic is compile-time-constant).

proof_on_hardware.path: Form kernel emits fam-exp-poly's 112-byte image -> form-macho mo-object-sym -> Mach-O .o (345 bytes) exporting _recipe -> ld -dylib + ad-hoc sign -> dlopen+call. No rustc/clang in the COMPUTE (clang only builds the dumb dlopen harness and the reference; the polynomial is the Form-emitted bytes).
proof_on_hardware.result: 10/10 on M4 (arm64 macOS). (A) BIT-EXACT vs the SAME degree-6 Horner in C (identical fma order over the identical pooled coefficients) at x in {0, 0.25, -0.3, 1e8, pi}, including fp-stress 1e8 -> 1.3888889722222266e+45 and irrational pi -> 22.188246569333078. (B) within ~1.3e-7 of libm exp() across the reduced interval [-ln2/2, ln2/2]: |err| = 0 at r=0, 1.245e-7 at r=+ln2/2 (the edge, bounded by r^7/7!), 1.142e-7 at r=-ln2/2, ~9e-10 at half-interval — a faithful exp(r) approximation on exactly the interval fam-exp-reduce guarantees.
proof_on_hardware.note: The exp(r) evaluator is now sovereign native bytes. With k=round (frintn), r (exp-reduce), 2^k (pow2), and now exp(r) (this) all on the M4 as Form-emitted bytes, the only thing left for a full native exp = 2^k * exp(r) is the composition + the k float->int cast (fa-fcvtzs).

no_regression: fam-horner-list / fam-exp-poly are purely additive to form-asm-matvec.fk (new recipes, no existing byte moved); the band and manifest row are additive. Sibling bands re-checked four-way after the shared-prelude edit: form-asm-pow2 -> 63, form-asm-exp-reduce -> 31, form-asm-exp-coef-pool -> 31, form-asm-matvec-2d -> 127, all 0 divergent.
design_note: Design-with-reference held (the relu slip's lesson — four-way agreement proves DETERMINISM, not correctness; only the M4-assembled oracle proves correctness): the exact degree-6 image AND a degree-2 image were assembled with clang -c on THIS M4 and read back out of the Mach-O __text section BEFORE authoring, pinning every byte and correcting the pool-stride assumption. The Form recipe's bytes were then verified byte-identical to both oracles through the Go kernel before the four-way run. READ-THE-BODY + uncontested-stone (lane-5): checked git branch -r + git log origin/main.. first — the recent form-asm-* claude branches are squash-merged source branches (content already on main via #3935/#3937/#3939/#3940), not in-flight; codex/full-real-llama-generation-d617 is the FULL-MODEL composition (its turf), whereas the atomic exp(r) polynomial evaluator was uncontested and was the exact stone fam-pow2's receipt named.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash) PLUS an on-M4 execution witness (form-macho -> ld -dylib -> dlopen, no rustc/clang in the compute). bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way + on-hardware observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) fam-exp end-to-end on bytes: fam-exp-reduce (k and r) + fa-fcvtzs (k float -> int) + fam-pow2 (2^k) + fam-exp-poly (exp(r)) + fmul, returning 2^k * exp(r), four-way + bit-exact vs libm exp within tolerance. (2) fam-softmax over ll-buffer: fam-max-loop (m = max_j) -> subtract-exp(fam-exp)-sum(fam-dot-loop)-normalize. (3) fam-gelu/fam-silu — fam-horner-list already IS their engine, over their own coefficient lists. (4) a minimax coefficient set (Remez) instead of Taylor for a tighter exp(r) on the same degree, when a lower error bar is wanted.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open: overall breathing, 0 recorded silences. Brick is host-independent (Form recipes + band + witness + receipt + manifest row); no deploy taken.
